title: alyssum halimifolium
intro: Alyssum halimifolium, also known as Sea Lavender or Trailing Alyssum, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and low-growing, spreading habit. Traditionally, Alyssum halimifolium has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and protect the skin from infections. The health benefits of Alyssum halimifolium include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially alleviating anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, Alyssum halimifolium is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support skin health and overall well-being.
